$comment: PE/COFF EXECUTABLE (PE32+) for x86_64 Windows. Second PE schema (plan 14 LK2 cycle 2, 2026-05-30). Consumed by `src/link/format/pe.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine. Differs from `pe64-x86_64-windows.format.json` (the .obj schema) in: `pe.type = exec`; sections carry `virtualAddress` (interpreted as RVA — offset from ImageBase); top-level `entryPoint` names the entry symbol (resolved against AssembledModule at link time); new `optionalHeader` block populated with IMAGE_OPTIONAL_HEADER64 fields. ImageBase = 0x140000000 (Windows x86_64 .exe convention per the MSVC linker default). Subsystem = IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_WINDOWS_CUI (3) — console subsystem; GUI exe would set 2. SectionAlignment = 0x1000 (4 KB page); FileAlignment = 0x200 (512 bytes, MSVC default).
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit Windows is LLP64 (int 32, long 32, long long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I32 here, the literal ladder + descriptor signatureByDataModel resolve their LLP64 arms. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: C bit-field allocation is OS/format-determined (the x86_64 CPU target serves BOTH this PE format and ELF-SysV), so the strategy lives here, not on the target. MSVC's x64 ABI starts a NEW allocation unit — aligned to the field's declared-type natural alignment — whenever an adjacent bit-field's declared-type SIZE differs from the current unit's type size (it never packs different-sized declared types into one unit), and the struct size covers the last unit's full declared-type width. Byte-ABI-exact to cl.exe (verified by the conformance witness): e.g. `{int a:1; char b:1;}` is sizeof 8 with b@byte4, vs gnu_packed's 4 with b@bit1.
$longDoubleFormatComment: FC17.9(e) D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE: MSVC x64 `long double` IS `double` (64-bit IEEE, 8/8) — the f64 axis collapses the c-subset `long double` row to F64, so the full double machinery serves it end-to-end (the LLP64 long==int identity-collapse precedent). OS/format-determined (the same x86_64 target serves ELF-SysV's x87-80), so the axis lives here next to dataModel.
$entryPointComment: Empty entryPoint defaults the walker to module.functions[0] (cycle-2 convention — same as LK1 ELF ET_EXEC). Real-name resolution closes with D-LK1-1 (HIR→LIR symbol-name thread). D-LK10-ENTRY Slice C (CLOSED 2026-06-02, commit 1a0b414): the linker prepends a synthetic `_start` trampoline as module.functions[0]; the schema's empty entryPoint resolves to functions[0] = the trampoline, which then calls the USER fn (the post-prepend functions[1+]). See plan 14 §2.13 — Stage 1 closed.
$externCallDispatchComment: PE resolves imports through IAT import THUNKS: the linker synthesizes one `jmp *[IAT slot]` code thunk per extern (the PE analog of an ELF PLT stub / Mach-O __stubs entry) and points each extern symbol's VA at its THUNK, so an extern reference — a direct call OR an address-taken value — is a callable code address. The call site is a plain direct `call rel32 -> thunk` (x86_64 E8 disp32); the thunk does the IAT indirection. `indirect-slot` (symbolVa = the raw .idata data slot) MISCOMPILES an address-taken import: `call *reg` through the slot executes data as code (sqlite os_win.c aSyscall[] — the pe64 leg's last run-green blocker). D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH + D-FFI-PE-IMPORT-THUNK.
$dataImportBindingComment: c149 (D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT, the PE half — the LAST missing image binding model): extern library DATA objects (msvcrt `_fmode` / `_environ`-class data exports) bind via the IAT SLOT ITSELF — PE `__imp_<name>` semantics: the loader import-resolves EVERY IAT slot to the exported symbol's ADDRESS (function or data alike), so for a data import the `.idata` slot IS a loader-filled pointer to the object. This is exactly the Mach-O `__got` non-lazy-pointer model — the SAME `got-indirect` enum value, no new vocabulary. The linker binds symbolVa[dataExtern] to the IAT slot VA (pe.cpp — FUNC externs → their `FF 25` .text THUNK VA per D-FFI-PE-IMPORT-THUNK, DATA externs → the `.idata` IAT slot VA; NO thunk is emitted for a data extern: a data object is not callable, and a data reference resolved to a thunk VA would read jump-stub BYTES as the object's value — the exact silent-miscompile class the linker's data-import gate guards). MIR→LIR materializes the object's address by lea-of-slot + deref (the shared GotIndirect lowering Mach-O uses — zero lowering changes). Contrast ELF exec's copy-relocation (the non-PIE .bss-copy model).
$processExitComment:
D-LK10-ENTRY Slice B (plan 14 §2.13): the Windows runnable-binary spine. Trampoline emitter issues a plain `call rel32` to kernel32.dll's ExitProcess THUNK (direct-plt, per D-FFI-PE-IMPORT-THUNK; was `call qword ptr [iat_ExitProcess]` under the retired indirect-slot model). The linker injects a synthetic `ExternImport{kernel32.dll, ExitProcess}` into module.externImports; the IAT writer (LK6 cycle 2a) emits the IAT slot, the import-thunk synthesis emits a `jmp *[iat_ExitProcess]` code thunk in .text, and the universal `call` LIR opcode emits E8 disp32 with a REL32 reloc on the disp32 patch site targeting the synthetic import's SymbolId (resolved to the thunk VA).

STDIO-FLUSH-AT-EXIT (2026-06-09) — WHY PE KEEPS ExitProcess (NOT msvcrt `exit`): the user mandate is 'terminate through a stdio-flushing libc-level primitive on every hosted format'. On elf/macho that meant switching from a raw exit syscall to libc `exit(3)`. On Windows, ExitProcess ALREADY flushes the msvcrt stdio buffers and is the canonical process-termination primitive: when ExitProcess runs, the loader sends DLL_PROCESS_DETACH to every loaded DLL in reverse-load order, and msvcrt.dll's detach handler flushes + closes its CRT streams. The `puts("hello")`-then-return corpus (examples/c-subset/shipped_include_puts, hello_puts) EMPIRICALLY prints 'hello
' on Windows under captured-pipe stdout — proving the buffered bytes reach stdout via this path (the 
 vs 
 is msvcrt text-mode CRLF translation, confirming the bytes flow through msvcrt's FILE layer). Switching PE to call msvcrt `exit` would be a regression risk for ZERO benefit (msvcrt `exit` ultimately calls the same kernel32 process-exit after the SAME stream cleanup) and would couple the entry spine to a specific CRT export. So the 'uniform libc exit' is realized PER FORMAT: elf→libc `exit`, macho→libSystem `exit` (symbol `_exit`), pe→kernel32 `ExitProcess` (which flushes via msvcrt DLL-detach). All three deliver buffered stdout before the process dies; the mechanism is config-driven per format, with NO format identity branch in the shared trampoline.
$processArgsComment: c111 (D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS): the Windows program-entry argc/argv mechanism. Unlike ELF/Mach-O (where the OS entry receives the argument vector on the stack / in registers), the PE OS entry gets NOTHING — the C runtime must ASK msvcrt for the arguments via an out-parameter export. `mechanism: crt-out-param` drives the MIR-tier `synthesizePeStartup`, which synthesizes a pre-main init `int _dss_pe_start()` that declares local argc/argv/env + a zeroed msvcrt `_startupinfo`, calls the CRT export with their ADDRESSES (the out-parameters), then forwards (argc, argv) to the user entry — and retargets the program entry to that synth function. WIDE vs NARROW is chosen by the RESOLVED ENTRY'S SIGNATURE, never a format flag: a `wmain(int, wchar_t**)` entry (argv element = pe wide-char u16) binds `crtWideArgvFn` (__wgetmainargs); a `main(int, char**)` entry binds `crtNarrowArgvFn` (__getmainargs) — so ONE descriptor serves both entry shapes. Both are real msvcrt.dll exports (dumpbin ord 168 / 139). The entry-trampoline emits NO argument materialization for this mechanism — the synth init owns it. Contrast ELF/AArch64's stack-vector arm above.
$entryCallingConventionComment: MS x64 calling convention — status (user fn return value in rax) is moved into argGprs[0] = rcx before the indirect ExitProcess call. Read via `target.callingConventionByName("ms_x64")` at trampoline-build time.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: D-LK2-RODATA (`.rdata`) + D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER (writable `.data` + uninitialized `.bss`) + D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL (thread-local `tdata`/`tbss`, TLS C3): the PE-Exec walker emits `.rdata` (read-only), `.data` (mutable initialized globals, MEM_WRITE), `.bss` (zero-init globals, CNT_UNINITIALIZED_DATA — SizeOfRawData=0), and `.tls` (the thread-local TEMPLATE) from `AssembledModule.dataItems` per the item's `section`. BOTH `tdata` (initialized `thread_local int g = 7;`) AND `tbss` (zero-init `thread_local int g;`) are advertised: PE folds a `tbss` item into the ONE `.tls` section's IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 `SizeOfZeroFill` tail (no separate `.tbss` section) — but a bare `thread_local int g;` IS a `tbss` item, so omitting `tbss` from this set would make the linker's pre-walker gate reject a valid program (M-1). The linker's gate consults this set per-item. Cross-format agnosticism: this is the SAME field ELF/Mach-O exec formats declare; zero linker source changes.
$relroExecComment: D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE (c145): `relro` (a CONST global carrying LOAD-TIME relocations — a const function-pointer table, e.g. sqlite os_win.c aSyscall[]) is advertised so the linker's pre-walker gate admits it. In the PE32+ image the walker FOLDS a relro item into the read-only `.rdata` section (PE base-relocates `.rdata` — the loader applies the IMAGE_REL_BASED_DIR64 base-relocation into the slot BEFORE the page is sealed read-only, exactly as it already does for F5 symbol-address global pointers), so NO separate relro section row is declared here — relro rides `.rdata` and its abs64 fixups join the existing `.reloc` DIR64 table.
$tlsAccessComment: TLS C3 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): how code reaches a thread-local object's per-thread copy on Windows x86_64 — the SAME x86 primitive ELF uses, config-only differences (model + segment + displacement). pe-indexed = the Windows TEB-slot-array model: `mov rax, gs:[0x58]` (segmentPrefixByte 101 = 0x65 gs-override, baseDisplacement 88 = 0x58 = TEB.ThreadLocalStoragePointer) reads the per-thread array of module TLS-block pointers; `mov ecx, [__dss_tls_index]` (the writer-minted module TLS index singleton — tlsIndexSlotName; a 32-BIT read, the loader fills the slot with this module's index) selects THIS module's block; `mov rax, [rax + rcx*8]` loads the block base; `lea rax, [rax + secrel32(sym)]` adds the variable's POSITIVE section-relative offset within the `.tls` template (the tls-tpoff32 reloc, kind 4 = IMAGE_REL_AMD64_SECREL here). The Windows loader processes IMAGE_DIRECTORY_ENTRY_TLS for the main thread BEFORE the entry point AND allocates+copies a fresh block per CreateThread — so per-thread storage is real. The SAME `tlsbase` opcode row serves ELF (fs) and PE (gs); a format WITHOUT this block fails loud K_FormatLacksThreadLocalSupport on the first thread-local access.
$librarySynthesisComment: D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO (opened for the pe/macho vehicle split): the Windows CRT exports NO C11 <threads.h> symbols, so each thrd_*/mtx_*/cnd_*/tss_* function is a COMPILER-SYNTHESIZED shim (src/mir/merge/synth_threads_shim.cpp, the `win32` vehicle) emitted over kernel32 CRITICAL_SECTION / CONDITION_VARIABLE / Fls* primitives. libraryPath is the DLL the on-demand kernel32 helpers import from — the value that DISSOLVES the pass's former hardcoded "kernel32.dll" literal (now config-sourced for BOTH vehicles). Keyed by the FORMAT; the synth pass dispatches on this declared verb, never on a format identity.
$artifactProfilesComment: Profiles this format SERVES (plan 06 AP3) — a runnable console executable ⇒ cli (the format-side twin of the language's artifactProfiles[]; driver fails loud D_ArtifactProfileFormatMismatch on a profile not in this set). A `gui` profile (IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_WINDOWS_GUI) would be added when gui codegen lands — same format, profile-selected subsystem/entry, zero gate-code change.

pe.$comment: IMAGE_FILE_HEADER identity. machine = IMAGE_FILE_MACHINE_AMD64 (0x8664). characteristics = IMAGE_FILE_EXECUTABLE_IMAGE (0x0002) | IMAGE_FILE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE (0x0020) = 0x0022. type = exec.

optionalHeader.$comment: IMAGE_OPTIONAL_HEADER64 (PE/COFF spec §3.4). magic = PE32+ (0x20B). imageBase = 0x140000000 (Windows x86_64 .exe default per MSVC). sectionAlignment = 0x1000 (4 KB virtual). fileAlignment = 0x200 (512 raw). subsystem = WINDOWS_CUI (3). dllCharacteristics = HIGH_ENTROPY_VA (0x0020) | DYNAMIC_BASE (0x0040) | NX_COMPAT (0x0100) | TERMINAL_SERVER_AWARE (0x8000) = 0x8160 (ASLR + DEP enabled). Stack/heap reserve = 1 MiB; commit = 4 KiB (MSVC defaults). OS/Subsystem versions = 6.0 (Windows Vista — minimum modern target).

sections.$comment: Executable code section. virtualAddress = 0x1000 — first page after the headers (which occupy [0, sectionAlignment) inside the ImageBase mapping). Characteristics = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_CODE (0x00000020) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_EXECUTE (0x20000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_ALIGN_16BYTES (0x00500000) = 0x60500020. Same value as the .obj schema's `.text` Characteristics (alignment + read/execute) — the PE32+ image just adds the RVA via the substrate `virtualAddress` field.
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only data section (D-LK2-RODATA closure). Emitted by the PE walker between .text and .idata when `AssembledModule.dataItems` carries any item with `section==Rodata`. virtualAddress=0 here is a placeholder — the walker computes the actual RVA at emit time as (textVirtualAddress + textVirtualSize) since sections must be contiguous in VA space. Characteristics = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_INITIALIZED_DATA (0x00000040) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) = 0x40000040 — initialized data, read-only (no write, no execute). The walker reads the section name + characteristics from this row; the VA is computed dynamically from layout.
sections.name: .rdata

sections.$comment: Writable initialized data (mutable globals) — D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER. Emitted by the PE walker after .rdata when AssembledModule.dataItems carries an item with section==Data. virtualAddress=0 is a placeholder — the walker computes the RVA at emit time (contiguous after .rdata). Characteristics (the substrate `type` field) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_INITIALIZED_DATA (0x00000040) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE (0x80000000) = 0xC0000040 = 3221225536 — initialized data, READ + WRITE (the MEM_WRITE bit is what makes a runtime store legal; a .rdata store faults). The walker reads name + characteristics from this row.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: Uninitialized data (tentative globals `int g;`) — D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER. Emitted by the PE walker after .data. SizeOfRawData=0 / PointerToRawData=0 (NO file footprint — the loader zero-fills VirtualSize bytes); contributes to SizeOfUninitializedData. virtualAddress=0 — walker-computed. Characteristics (`type`) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_UNINITIALIZED_DATA (0x00000080) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE (0x80000000) = 0xC0000080 = 3221225600 — uninitialized data, READ + WRITE.
sections.name: .bss

sections.$comment: Thread-local TEMPLATE section (`thread_local` — D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL, TLS C3). ONE `.tls` section carries the INITIALIZED per-thread template bytes (the `tdata` items); a zero-init `thread_local int g;` (`tbss`) item contributes NO file bytes — it is folded into the IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 `SizeOfZeroFill` tail, so there is NO separate `.tbss` section (this `kind: tdata` row is looked up by the walker for the SECTION NAME + Characteristics; the tbss span rides the directory). The walker also lays the 40-byte IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 + the 4-byte `_tls_index` slot at the tail of THIS section (both past the template span [StartAddressOfRawData, EndAddressOfRawData) so they are never per-thread-copied); a writable section is a valid home for the loader-read directory AND the loader-written index. Characteristics (`type`) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_INITIALIZED_DATA (0x40) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE (0x80000000) = 0xC0000040 = 3221225536 — the loader applies base relocations into the template + directory VA fields (requires MEM_WRITE) and each thread's copy is writable. virtualAddress=0 — walker-computed (contiguous after the prior data section).
sections.name: .tls

relocations.$comment: PE32+ image-side relocations: same kinds as the .obj schema. cycle-2 walker applies intra-module relocs in-place via the LK6 cycle 1 structured-formula triple (closes D-LK1-3-equivalent for PE). REL32 is PC-relative 32-bit; ADDR64/ADDR32 are absolute. The image is ASLR-aware (dllCharacteristics carries DYNAMIC_BASE), so the walker DOES emit a `.reloc` base-relocation section carrying an IMAGE_REL_BASED_DIR64 entry per 8-byte absolute fixup: the cross-CU thunk slots + F5 symbol-address global pointers + (TLS C3) the 3 IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 VA fields.
relocations.name: IMAGE_REL_AMD64_REL32

relocations.$comment: TLS C3 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): the thread-local SECTION-RELATIVE fixup — the disp32 of `lea rax, [rax + secrel32(sym)]`, the FINAL instruction of the pe-indexed access sequence. kind=4 is the SHARED target-schema `tls-tpoff32` row (Linear, width 4, absolute, tls:true) — x86_64 serves BOTH ELF and PE from ONE `[reg, symbol]` lea variant, so the ELF↔PE split lives HERE at the FORMAT level (this row) not on the target (mirrors elf64's kind-4 `R_X86_64_TPOFF32`). The walker patches the 4 bytes from its POSITIVE templateOffset-valued symbolVa entry (the variable's byte offset within the `.tls` template — NOT the ELF NEGATIVE tpoff), so `blockBase + templateOffset` = the per-thread address; the image carries NO runtime reloc for this disp32 (it is an ASLR-invariant relative offset, a link-time constant). nativeId = IMAGE_REL_AMD64_SECREL (11) — meaningful only for a future .obj emission of this kind; the row's existence satisfies the linker's pre-walker cross-reference unifier + the walker's in-arm tls-kind check (the CRIT-1 XOR backstop).
relocations.name: IMAGE_REL_AMD64_SECREL
